data.title: Investors moving to natural gas, away from oil as 2025 energy stock play
data.description:
Crude oil futures ended fractionally higher Tuesday, with the market weighing the Middle East situation after the fall of the Assad regime in Syria, expectations of Chinese monetary stimulus and the path of U.S. interest rates.

While Syria is not a major oil producer, it is strategically located and has had strong ties with Russia and Iran.

The Middle East actually could become "less influential" for oil prices after Assad's downfall, XS.com analyst Samer Hasn said, with the "potential diminishing of the risk premium around disruptions to crude flows in the region."

"Syria was the most important corridor to bypass U.S. sanctions and a destination for Iranian oil exports," Hasn wrote, so "this structural shift may make Iran less willing to escalate in the region, which may entail risks to the safety of crude flows, which are now tighter for Iran."

Wednesday's OPEC monthly report "could offer additional guidance that could tilt bearish if the organization opts to adjust oil demand lower toward the IEA's forecast that has been offering an entirely different picture of global oil demand going forward," Ritterbusch analysts said, adding that "the geopolitical factor that has been spiking oil values occasionally during the past couple of years is beginning to wear thin as a pricing influence."

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for January delivery closed +0.3% at $68.59/bbl, and front-month February Brent (CO1:COM) ended +0.1% at $72.19/bbl, while January Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) settled -0.6% to $3.163/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (NYSEARCA:UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Oil names are out and gas names are in among energy investors, as traders increasingly bet on gains in gas to fuel their portfolios more than crude, Bloomberg reports.

The First Trust Natural Gas ETF (FCG) has enjoyed nine straight weeks of fund inflows, the longest streak since 2021; Comstock Resources (CRK) is the fund's top performer during the period, jumping 22%, and EQT (EQT) and Gulfport Energy (GPOR) have gained more than 15% over the same period.

J.P. Morgan analyst Arun Jayaram predicts a "role reversal" next year, in which investors should favor natural gas names over oil stocks, given expectations for an oversupplied oil market and “tailwinds” supporting the gas producers such as a boost in LNG exports from North America and rising demand for gas-fired power demand by data centers.

J.P. Morgan's favorite natgas producers to play the theme are Antero Resources (AR), EQT (EQT) and Gulfport (GPOR).

data.title: Gold's long-term upside outlook still intact, Citi says
data.description:
Gold futures jumped Tuesday to the highest close in more than two weeks, after China's central bank restarted gold purchases for the first time in six months and the Chinese government pledged to adopt an "appropriately loose" monetary policy and a more proactive fiscal approach next year.

The People's Bank of China bought around five metric tons of gold in November, a relatively small amount compared with previous purchases of up to 30 tons/month, but the resumption of purchasing at all is the key factor in gold's latest rally, Commerzbank analysts said.

The buying may be in response to Donald Trump's election victory, which threatens China with the potential introduction of punitive tariffs, Commerzbank said, also noting the price of gold had dropped significantly after a series of records, which may have stimulated buying interest.

For gold to add to gains, central bank purchases would need to continue in the coming months, the bank said.

Also, expectations have solidified among traders that the Federal Reserve will cut U.S. interest rates later this month, enhancing the appeal of non-interest bearing bullion, according to ActivTrades analyst Ricardo Evangelista.

The spotlight will move to the U.S. Consumer Price Index on Wednesday and the Producer Price Index on Thursday, both considered key in shaping the Federal Reserve's rate-cut decisions.

With two U.S. rate cuts so far this year, traders predict an 86% chance of another 25-bp cut at the Fed's meeting next week, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for December delivery ended +1.2% to $2,697.60/oz, its best settlement value since November 22, and front-month December silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +0.4% to $32.363/oz, the highest since November 5.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Gold's long-term upside remains intact, Citi analysts said, supported by ongoing deterioration of the U.S. labor market, still-elevated interest rates weighing on growth, and higher demand for ETFs.

Long-term themes including rising global debt levels amid continued "de-dollarization" also are structurally positive for gold, Citi said, maintaining price targets of $2,800/oz for up to three months and $3,000/oz for 6-12 months.

data.title: Figs jumps after report of $1B takeover offer from PE firm
data.description:
Medical-apparel maker Figs (NYSE:FIGS) soared 9.2% in after hours trading on a report that private-equity firm Story3 Capital Partners made a more than $1 billion takeover offer.

Story3 offered $6 a share for Figs (FIGS), according to a WSJ report on Tuesday, which cited a letter viewed by the publication. The price represents a 17% premium to FIGS closing price on Tuesday.

Story3 and affiliated funds own a little more than 1% stake in FIGS, according to the report.

Story3 said Fortress Investment Group, one of its strategic limited partners, is prepared to provide the debt financing for a transaction, according to the letter.

Figs has a $889 million market cap and its shares have dropped 26% this year. The scrub maker's stock has dropped 77% since going public in May 2021.

data.title: Daktronics gains as activist investor Alta Fox plans to nominate directors
data.description:
Daktronics (NASDAQ:DAKT) rose 5.3% as activist investor Alta Fox Capital Management said it plans to nominate director candidates for the board.

Alta Fox, which is Daktronics (DAKT) largest holder with a ~12% stake, believes the company trades at a “governance discount” due to its staggered board, poison pill and other issues, Alta Fox founder and managing partner Connor Haley said at the Bloomberg Activism Forum in New York City on Tuesday.

"DAKT has underperformed its potential over the last decade, operating like a private family business with little accountability to shareholders," Alta Fox wrote in its investor presentation.

Alta Fox, which has been an investor in Daktronics (DAKT) since 2022, believes that the shares may have 100% upside to $40 a share by FY28 with changes to its operations and investor communications.

data.title: American Lithium announces intention to voluntarily delist from Nasdaq
data.description:
• American Lithium (NASDAQ:AMLI) board has approved the voluntary delisting of its common shares from Nasdaq and the deregistration with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • The company anticipates that it will file with the SEC a Form 25, relating to the delisting and deregistration on or about December 20, 2024, with the delisting of shares taking effect ten calendar days thereafter.
  • The last trading day of the American Lithium shares on the Nasdaq will be December 27, 2024.
  • The AMLI shares will continue their listing on the TSX Venture Exchange and the Frankfurt Stock Exchange.
  • AMLI shares were up over 4.5% in after hours trading on Tuesday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: VIQ Solutions announces retirement of COO Susan Sumner
data.description:
• VIQ Solutions (TSX:VQS:CA) on Tuesday announced the retirement of Susan Sumner as chief operating officer (COO) and president of the company.
  • Sumner will continue as a part-time advisor to the Company until January 31, 2025. In connection with her retirement, Sumner has informed the company that she will resign from her director position on the board of directors of the company as of January 31, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 1911 Gold upsizes non-brokered private placement to $8.1M
data.description:
• 1911 Gold Corporation (TSXV:AUMB:CA) upsized its previously announced non-brokered private placement offering to now raise aggregate gross proceeds of up to $8.1M.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about December 23, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cellectar stock plunges 50% post-market on restructuring news
data.description:
Cellectar Biosciences (NASDAQ:CLRB) stock plunged 50% post-market after the company announced it plans to lay off around 60% of its workforce as it seeks a partner or buyer for its iopofosine |131 clinical program.

The biotech company said it expects the layoffs to be completed by the end of the year. The restructuring should extend its cash runway into Q3 2025.

Cellectar said it made the decision after determining it would have to run a confirmatory study aimed at generating progression-free survival data in order to seek accelerated approval for iopofonise | 131 for Waldenstrom’s macroglobulinemia.

It added that iopofonise | 131 would be better brought to market by a larger organization with more resources and plans to pursue strategic options for the program, according to a statement.

Cellectar said it plans to focus its resources on advancing its radiopharmaceutical drug candidates CLR-121225 and CLR-121125 into Phase 1 testing for solid tumor cancers. It intends to file Investigational New Drug applications with the FDA for both candidates in the first half of 2025.

data.title: Stitch Fix shares rally as FQ2 loss narrows, outlook beats street view
data.description:
With the discontinuation of its UK business and increased sales per customer, Stitch Fix (NASDAQ:SFIX) realized a narrower loss in the fiscal first quarter, beating Wall Street’s expectations and launching shares more than 22% higher in after-hours trading.

The $0.05 loss per share – versus a loss of $0.22 per share a year ago – was achieved despite a 12.6% drop in sales and 18.6% loss in active clients from a year ago. Net revenue per active client, however, increased 5% year-over-year to $531 and cost of goods sold decreased by 15%.

The improvement towards profitability coupled with a wider profit market encouraged a bullish outlook for the fiscal second quarter and for fiscal 2025.

“Our fiscal year is off to a strong start. We exceeded our expectations in the first quarter on the top and bottom lines,” said CEO Matt Baer. “Our clients are responding to the newness we have brought to our assortment, as well as the improvements we’ve made to our client experience. This progress is a testament to the Stitch Fix team’s ongoing execution of our transformation strategy, and we continue to expect to return to revenue growth by the end of FY26.”

For the fiscal second quarter, Stitch Fix (SFIX) expects revenue to decline by 9% to 12% to be within a range of $290M to $300M, exceeding expectations of $283.6M. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between $8M and $13M.

For the fiscal year ending July 31, the company expects revenue to decline by 10%-13% to be within a range of $1.14B and $1.18B. This compares to the consensus estimate of $1.14B. Adjusted EBITDA is targeted for $25M to $36M. Gross margin for both FQ2 and FY25 is to be approximately 44% to 45%.
